content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains an integer N, the number of dolls. The next N lines are the types of dolls that are left.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, display the type of doll that doesn't have a pair, in a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For this case, we have three dolls [1, 2, 1]. So we have two dolls of type 1, but only one doll of type 2. Thus one doll of type 2 is missing.
For this case [1, 1, 2, 2, 3], we have two dolls of type 1 and 2, but only one doll of type 3. Thus one doll of type 3 is missing.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using the XOR operation. The XOR operation has the property that a ^ a = 0 and a ^ 0 = a. So, if we XOR all the numbers in the array, the numbers that appear twice will cancel each other out and the number that appears once will be left.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of dolls in each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — as we are not using any extra space that scales with the input size.

code.Python3:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    ans = 0
    for _ in range(N):
        num = int(input())
        ans ^= num
    print(ans)
